text: Ryan asks a lot of you as a student but does so in a genuine effort to teach you the material. Daily reading quizzes for attendance, you *do* have to do the readings, lots of weight put on participation in class. A group presentation and final solo essay. I learned so, so much and stayed in touch with him after the class :)

unclear criteria for grading but it wasnt super bad/difficult so as long as u remember the main ideas of each reading, you should be fine. it reading can be a lot sometimes and the textbooks are pricey tho so :[ there's a quiz each week but not hard. there is a group project which comes quick so be ready

My fav professor ever! he cares about his students and fosters an academic environment. Very gracious on grading as long as you show effort and understanding. Great at answering questions and very understanding. Class was a lot of fun and I felt immersed even when the topic was serious

Professor Coonerty was the best teacher I have ever had in my entire life. I have never learned so much in a class before. He helped us get so engaged with his courses and even get excited to come to class and learn from him. He is so easy to talk to if you need help and was so accommodating, accepting and supportive of all of his students.

The most insightful class and experience I have had. Ryan has provided me more insight into the legal field. There is a decent amount of reading, but it is within reason. Reasonable quizzes, a group project, and a very reasonable final assignment. Ryan is an amazing and fun professor here. Take his class.
Great teacher

One of the best professors I've had, really cares about his students. Ryan has a lot of experience in the legal and political atmosphere, which makes for great anecdotes. There isn't much structure to the lecture, but he is very engaging, just be ready to take notes. Keep up with readings and the Times, although quizzes aren't graded super hard.

Ryan is literally my favorite professor in LGST department. He doesn't lecture at us the whole time which is really nice, instead he encourages class participation which is very engaging. There are daily quizzes (that are super easy, just do the readings and you'll be fine). His office hours are always super helpful too.

The class is kinda disorganized since he's just talking and there's no powerpoint/lectures but he is REALLY easygoing and lenient and that definitely makes up for it. I hated the daily quizzes but they force you to have at least some idea of what's going on in the readings and in the world (he tests on current events in the New York Times).

My advice: Coonerty does not lecture off of slides, so be prepared to take good notes based off of in-class discussions. Grading on quizzes is lenient(mostly to account for attendance). Be able to argue and provide evidence to differing opinions on exams (the more clear and concise you are the better). Participate in class.
The TA seemed to be very generous with grading. The professor is easy going too. It sounds like some people are just salty about their grades. Exams and essay prompts were straightforward.

My fav class at UCSC! Little structure to lectures, but he utilizes discussions to guide us in formulating our own conclusions by providing materials+overarching questions. His style made me engaged and want to work hard. Passed everyone on papers but few got straight A's. I think daily quizzes only count for participation, so do well on papers.

Professor Coonerty is a good guy and offers an interesting perspective as an educator. While none of the three courses I took with him were highly structured, they still touched on important events happening in the status quo and how these events relate back to themes of American political history. All of his courses also had great guest lectures.

Great lecturer. There are 2-3 question quizzes on the assigned reading at the beginning of every class but he lets you miss two (he won't tell you this) and he'll be lenient on grading those quizzes if you participate in class. The reader is usually mostly New York Times and case briefs so if you can't afford it just google the article/case.

I really recommend that if you have a chance to take one of his classes you do. Its worth the work, which really isn't that much. There are quizzes on the reading at the start of the class, but it really isn't that bad. You actually end up learning something unlike other classes.  As well as the quality of his teaching is remarkable. MUST TAKE.

Such an awesome professor! Two time mayor of Santa Cruz and now on the Board of Supervisors, he has a ton of connections and is uniquely qualified to teach politics here in Santa Cruz. If you participate in class and attend lectures, the class isn't too hard. Lectures are actually interesting, and you'll find yourself wanting to come to class!

Amazing professor! Very detailed and thoughtful lectures. He can really get a discussion going for the whole class, it's important to participate! If you have the chance to take him, totally do! The short quizzes at the beginning of class are col because they keep the pressure to keep up with readings and count towards attendance!

I really enjoyed his interactive approach to teaching. Instead of lecturing for an hour and 45 minutes, he allowed students to give their own insight into the relevant course topics, which really enlivened the classroom. If you want to be an active participant and learn to engage with material as it applies to the real world, take him!
Easy. Got to class, read cases. Ryan is really fun to listen to.

One of the best professors I've had. Really changes the way you think, I recommend taking any class by him. He makes you read the NY Times which seems difficult at first but is so valuable, still read it to this day

My very first Upper Division class.  Although challenging, he really brings out the best in students, challenges us - makes us think outside the box.  Perhaps cliche, its the truth.  He doesn't want students to believe what professors tells us, he wants them to find out for themselves.  Amazing, BEST PROFESSOR I've had at UCSC. Thanks Ryan.
Ryan is AMAZING! Best professor ever. Is there anything he can't do or doesn't know!? He give practical and real advice. He's smart and genuinely cares about his students. And such a cutie!

I have taken two courses with Professor Coonerty. His classes are not easy, but he is knowledgeable and will always go well out of his way to make sure you know the material. His lectures are always interesting, mostly because he is hilarious when you listen to what he says. Take a class with him, he is a staple of the UCSC Legal Studies program.
Coonerty is AWESOME. He's super interesting, clear about the topics and very knowledgeable. He's also hilarious. His class led me to switch majors.

Ryan is very personable and helpful. Class material is very dense and far from easy, but Ryan makes instruction to be an exciting, relevant, and worthy experience. Highly recommended. Shaped my decision to attend Law School.

Taken Coonerty 2 times now and I really enjoy his class. Hes a very smart guy and knows a lot about the subjects hes teaching and a very influential person. If I could I would probably take every class at UCSC with him. Doesnt mean hes easy, its because he knows how to reach out.  Clas requires a good amount of ready if you want to succeed.take him
He's a good professor and very willing to help out his students.  I enjoyed his class a lot.
even though i failed coonerty's class i do feel that he's a smart person so I would recommend his class

Ryan is a great professor! If you can?t handle reading the newspaper - then you don?t belong in college anyways. Take advantage of his office hours ? he is a great resource: very knowledgeable and willing to help. Has a great sense of humor; his laugh will undoubtedly bring a smile to your face ? it?s the cutest thing I have ever heard...

I took 3 classes over the years and overall, though I like him as a person, he's really a terrible professor. Echo to the negative comments - frequently cancelled lecturers, begins late, ends early, i've covered a terms worth of his material in the intro to other classes with rigorous professors, no joke. High school level teaching - easy easy A.

If you're thinking about law school be prepared for him to challenge your desire to actually pursue a JD. Knows his stuff. The lectures are extremely entertaining if you are interested in the law. Those who aren't I find have a hard time following. His lectures were very clearly organized. Tests were very fair and challenged you to think. Take good notes and you'll mostly be okay.

If you're thinking about law school be prepared for him to challenge your desire to actually pursue a JD. Knows his stuff. The lectures are extremely entertaining if you are interested in the law. Those who aren't I find have a hard time following. His lectures were very clearly organized. Tests were very fair and challenged you to think. Take good notes and you'll mostly be okay.
Easy A, just read. fair grader. interesting topics.

Ryan is a fantastic professor.  If you want to learn how the real world of law works take his class.  You have to read in order to do well, which based on the other comments, you guys didn't do.  Not hot, but cute. . .

Ryan Coonerty was by far the worst professor i have ever had. He spent all of his time sharing his philosophies instead of actually teaching. The rest of the lectures were filled with Guests, and Ryan did nothing. Thanks a lot Coonerty.

I didn't care for him as a professor. Lectures were frequently canceled and there were far too many guest lecturers. He spent a ton of time on the news, which was interesting, but sometimes overwhelmed the actual material. The tests were completely random- poorly written and rarely on what he said they would be. Seemed smug and full of himself.

CIVIL LIBERTIES with Ryan is a must! He's such an awesome professor. His lectures are easy to follow along, he has a boyish charm to his teachings, open to student's opinions. He definitely makes class enjoyable.

Fun and interesting professor. Very funny in class but sometimes doesn't actually talk about anything else but the news. He does a lot better with smaller classes so this was not his best class. A little frustrating to keep up with the news and to what extend it is tested on on exams.
fun teacher, tests mostly from class discussions.  easy, interesting and fun.
Forces his opinions where they have no place in the curriculum. He is a great teacher though! Clear, fair, knowledgeable and helpful. Take his classes if you can!

kind of rambles about the news which is great... keeps you up to speed. however, you get sort of lost in the humble jumble of the endless discussions. when you get to the test you're really confused on what exactly you're being tested on. however he's totally dreamy. he's such a goober but really sexy. brains are hot. i hope hes not married.
Great teacher, very knowledgable, great sense of humor, the cutest slightly dorkish laugh EVER.

Ryan is a great guy and a good teacher.  He is funny and approachable.  However, I don't get all the comments about how hot he is.  If he's hot, I must be a Greek God.hmmmm....maybe I am a Greek God.  Wait a minute...Ryan, have you been rating yourself the whole time?????? I know you mentioned these salacious remarks in class!!!
LOVE HIM! interesting, cute, funny, knowledgeable, overall an  amazing class and professor! Everyone should take this class. I am looking forward to taking many more classes from him!
great lecturer, though he gets kind of snarky if no one participates. the reader was huge, but the reading was fantastic and definitely interesting. oh, and he's definitely hot in a nerdy way.
Great teacher..Good sense of humor, very helpful and really hot..

Alert: Studies have shown that starting and ending your sentences with 'New York Times' increases both your grade and # of times he checks you out in class  threefold compared to students who only cite the Weekly Standard, WSJ and the Washington Times, showing his liberal bias.

Great professor; young and knows a lot about current world affairs. He's also a great resource, very informative in helping with the LSAT's and general legal issues. Invited the whole class to Costa Brava for drinks after the final (this was during summer session).
Professor Coonerty is freaking awesome. He's great at law and very helpful.
Great professor! Has a really good background in the law too.
Ryan is such an amazing professor. He is great at keeping the class up to speed with relevant news stories and he always has a story from his experiences to share.
ryan is very down to earth and approachable. he's a great lecturer because he tells you what he wants you to know from the reading in plain english, not huge technical words.

this class is not for paper writers. if you're good at tests, this is the class for you. if you're not a human encyclopedia...not the best choice in classes. i took his courses during the summer and they were hard to manage with everything else going on. regular sessions should be pretty chill, though. oh, and by the way, if you you think brains are hot, he's delicious.
His teaching style was basically that he relied on the kids in the class to take up the entire classtime. The class was a joke, but in hindsight I learned SO much. Thanks classmates haha.
No Comments

This was my first law class, and i loved it. Prof. Coonerty did a great job engaging the class and making it interestiing and fun to go to. He did a great job of getting everyone interested and setting an open environmet for students to talk. Very approachable and nice.
Tight.

He was super helpful in all aspects.  His lectures are interesting and I like how he brings in videos and guest speakers.  Tests are a bit hard but otherwise everything was fine.  I would definitely take another class with him.  Not horrible looking yall.
I just love this guy! This is the second course I've taken with him and I just think he's awesome. =)
Are you kidding me? Easy on the eyes? You guys saying that because other legal studies profs. are like, 60 years old? Great teacher.  NOT easy on the eyes.

Cool guy who knew the material well despite his age, but often ran out of things to say and frequently let the class out early.  If you're not a good test taker don't take his classes because your whole grade is based on four tricky true false tests, and if you take it, make sure you read NYTIMES.

He's a cool guy and knows his stuff. If you know anything about current affairs this class is no problem. Lots of extra credit. Got over 100% on everything and still only got an A. No A+? WTF mate? Law schools eat the A+ stuff up Ryan!! Come on now.

Ryan is an excellent profesor. He invokes the desire the explore the subject matter further and provides resources to do so in the community and beyond. He is a captivating lecturer, and he's easy on the eyes.

Ryan's classes are very good if you like you like to get up close and personal with the underworkings of government policies, processes agencies. The reading is moderate and interseting. The NYT test questions  are a bit annoying. Ryan is young, friendly and oh yeah, hes hot;) just a bonus girls!

Ryan, goes above and beyond. He truly does show a genuine interest in the students success. Easy to talk to. He is the type of professor that you can count on as a resource even after you've taken one of his classes. I loved it all, I would definetly take another course with him!
Ryan is great. He is always willing to help everyone.

Ryan's a sweetheart, but a lousy professor. No papers, which can be a mixed blessing, but if you're not one for memorizing information and spitting it back out, DON'T BOTHER! Easy in other people's minds, but certainly not in mine. Not up to par with the Legal Studies department. Stupid NY Times!
Great teacher. he knows his stuff and hes very fair in all aspects. i actually enjoy going to this class because its stuff thats happening everyday. hes great at answering law-related questions.
A nice guy and good teacher. Liked the guest lectures and the NY Times. Not too difficult but you learn. Good if you are interested in Law school.

The lectures as well as the reading s proved to be very interesting. Ryan covered, the topics you would expect to study during the first year of law school. The quizzes were pretty fair, and the guest lecturers proved to be entertaining.
